title: Release-channel policy: preview eligibility for all buildable products

unblock_all_bundles_decision.title: Hardware/bench/compliance/commercial blockers are STABLE-only; they never block PREVIEW.
unblock_all_bundles_decision.summary: Formalises the decision that every bundle / product firmware target which is buildable (or can be made buildable) is preview-eligible, including the fan-control and TRIAC paths. A blocker that rests only on missing hardware proof, missing bench evidence, missing compliance evidence, or missing commercial availability gates STABLE / full release ONLY. It must NOT block a preview artifact for a buildable target. The only thing that can stop an actual preview cut is a genuine BUILDABILITY blocker (e.g. HW-005 GPIO routing for TRIAC). Nothing here becomes stable, recommended, default, buyable, or compliance-certified; Simple install stays the stable Bathroom PoE build only; the advanced / manual preview lane is the only allowed exposure path for risky or unverified builds.

revision_note: RELEASE-PREVIEW-WEBFLASH-ALL-BUILDABLE-001 aligns the concrete targets (config/preview-release-targets.json) with this policy: every buildable product is a preview / advanced-preview RELEASE target. It records the concrete delivery lanes (see delivery_lanes): SELV WebFlash targets use the 'webflash' lane; FanRelay / FanPWM / FanDAC use the 'manual-preview' lane; FanTRIAC uses the 'advanced-manual-preview' lane. The acknowledgement-gated / acknowledgement-gated-advanced webflash_exposure_class values on the fan / TRIAC matrix rows are the EVENTUAL WebFlash import target, not current state: WebFlash one-click import stays a separate, controlled follow-up until the WebFlash warning UX supports fan / TRIAC preview exposure. No product is promoted to stable and no new evidence is claimed.
description: Machine-readable release-channel policy. Defines the stable / preview / advanced-preview channel tiers and the rules that gate each tier, plus the preview-release eligibility matrix for every buildable Sense360 firmware target. Lack of hardware proof blocks STABLE only; it does not block PREVIEW artifact eligibility. This file is policy + eligibility metadata. It is read by tests/test_release_channel_policy.py.

scope.notes: Docs/config only. This PR records eligibility and policy. Actual preview artifact publication (a webflash-builds.json entry + wrapper + release tag) is a separate, explicitly scoped follow-up per target. config/webflash-builds.json remains the sole release-eligibility source of truth.

preview.label: Preview
preview.meaning: Buildable and installable for testers. NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, NOT a customer default.

advanced-preview.label: Advanced preview
advanced-preview.meaning: Preview plus stronger warnings for risky / mains-voltage installs. Competent-person, manual-install only. Same non-stable / non-recommended / non-default guarantees as preview, with mandatory mains-risk warning copy.

delivery_lanes.webflash: Preview / advanced-preview artifact published as a WebFlash-importable build (config/webflash-builds.json row + products/webflash wrapper + recorded build proof) behind the acknowledgement gate. config/webflash-builds.json is the sole WebFlash release-eligibility source of truth.
delivery_lanes.manual-preview: Buildable PREVIEW artifact for FanRelay / FanPWM / FanDAC, released to testers via the manual-preview lane (config/manual-firmware-artifacts.json). A real preview release target, not a passive candidate. WebFlash one-click import stays gated by the fan-token guardrail until the WebFlash warning UX supports fan preview exposure (separate follow-up); the preview artifact itself is releasable.
delivery_lanes.advanced-manual-preview: Advanced (mains-risk) PREVIEW artifact lane for FanTRIAC: manual-preview plus the mandatory mains-risk warning and competent-person manual install. Preview is allowed in principle; only an explicit build blocker (HW-005 buildability) prevents an actual cut. NOT a 'blocked-from-preview' lane. WebFlash import stays gated behind the advanced acknowledgement UX (separate follow-up).

experimental_lane.label: Experimental (self-build mains boards)
experimental_lane.meaning: Publish lane for firmware whose target hardware is a mains-touching board (S360-320 TRIAC, S360-310 Fan Relay, S360-400 240v PSU) that Sense360 NEVER places on the market: not sold assembled, not sold as a kit of parts, not sold as a populated PCB, not physically bundled in any kit, order, or promotion. Design files and firmware are published open-source under CERN-OHL-P; customers who build these boards do so entirely from their own sourcing, as self-builders of their own devices, at their own risk.

preconditions.functional_bench_protocol_complete: PACKAGE-TRIAC-001-class operator bench protocol completed on the real load for the target board
preconditions.signed_operator_attestation_committed: the signed operator attestation must be committed to the bench-proof container (e.g. docs/package-triac-001-operator-bench-proof.md) before lane entry
preconditions.note: Bench protocol completion proves function only (zero-cross lock, gate timing, load waveform, thermal soak, boot/stability, full-composition re-confirm). It is NOT a safety, EMC, CE / UKCA, or compliance claim of any kind.

experimental_lane.warning_copy: EXPERIMENTAL — SELF-BUILD MAINS HARDWARE. This firmware targets an open-source mains-voltage board design that Sense360 does not sell, supply, or place on the market in any form. If you build this board you are the manufacturer of your own device and act entirely at your own risk. Functional bench completion is NOT a safety, EMC, or compliance certification. Mains wiring must only be performed by a competent person. Incorrect installation can cause fire, electric shock, or death.
experimental_lane.target_assignment: TRIAC-COMMISSIONING-001 (the human-reviewed commissioning PR) assigned the first target — FanTRIAC / Ceiling-POE-VentIQ-FanTRIAC-RoomIQ — into this lane and made the build-channel suffix decision: build_channel is 'experimental' (artifact suffix -experimental, e.g. Sense360-Ceiling-POE-VentIQ-FanTRIAC-RoomIQ-v1.0.0-experimental.bin), emitted by scripts/derive_release_version_channel.py for a v{VERSION}-experimental prerelease tag and admitted in config/webflash-compatibility.json allowed_channels. The channel_tiers map is intentionally NOT extended (it stays stable / preview / advanced-preview); FanTRIAC keeps channel_tier advanced-preview while building on the experimental channel. The commissioning added the config/webflash-builds.json experimental row + kept the products/webflash wrapper and flipped config/product-catalog.json to status preview (channel experimental, webflash_build_matrix true), citing the attested PACKAGE-TRIAC-001 proof (docs/package-triac-001-operator-bench-proof.md) and this resolution record; it did NOT cut a release tag. The TRIAC-PUBLISH-ADVANCED-PREVIEW-001 publish gate in config/room-bundle-fan-variants.json is re-keyed by this same commissioning PR (its only permitted editor) to executed-via-experimental-lane, keeping every never-stable / never-recommended / never-default / never-buyable / never-kit tooth. Downstream WebFlash one-click import stays the separate WF-IMPORT-TRIAC-001 follow-up, unblocked only once the experimental release is cut.
experimental_lane.reopen_trigger: Any future act of placing any mains-touching board on the market reopens COMPLIANCE-001 and requires an external safety and EMC assessment BEFORE that act (indicative path: pre-compliance scan, then test-house safety + EMC). See docs/decisions/COMPLIANCE-001-RESOLUTION-001.md.

warning_copy.preview: PREVIEW FIRMWARE — buildable and installable for testers only. This build is NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, and NOT a customer default. No bench evidence and no compliance is claimed. Flash at your own risk and expect to recover with the rescue/stable firmware.
warning_copy.advanced-preview: ADVANCED PREVIEW — MAINS-VOLTAGE RISK. This firmware drives mains-voltage hardware (e.g. TRIAC phase-control dimming) and is for competent persons performing a manual installation only. It is NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, and is NEVER a default. No bench evidence and no electrical-safety / compliance certification is claimed. Incorrect installation can cause fire, electric shock, or death. Do not install unless you are qualified to work on mains wiring.

preview_release_matrix.notes: Existing live stable (currently v1.0.4). Stable path is unchanged by this policy. A preview successor (e.g. a future v1.x preview) is permitted under the preview tier but is not published in this PR.

Promoted to stable v1.0.6 under owner risk-acceptance waiver HW-AIRIQ-WAIVER-2026-06 (AirIQ sensor-stack bench accuracy not verified; S360-410 PoE PSU covered by HW-S360-410-WAIVER-2026-06). Owner waiver, not hardware verification. Bundle stays hidden / not buyable / not a customer default. AirIQ/VentIQ remain mutually exclusive.

Promoted to stable v1.0.5 under owner risk-acceptance waiver HW-S360-410-WAIVER-2026-06 (S360-410 PoE PSU cataloged_unverified; on-device flash verification not yet performed). Owner waiver, not hardware verification. Bundle stays hidden / not buyable / not a customer default.

preview_release_matrix.stable_blocker: S360-410 PoE-PSU schematic verification + LED preview-to-stable gauntlet
preview_release_matrix.notes: Living-room target. RoomIQ + LED ring (SELV). A dedicated product/wrapper YAML is still required before a preview artifact can be cut. Preview-eligible once that exists; not hardware verified.

preview_release_matrix.stable_blocker: S360-410 PoE-PSU schematic verification + LED preview-to-stable gauntlet
preview_release_matrix.notes: Corridor target shares the Living-room Ceiling-POE-RoomIQ-LED firmware. Preview-eligible once a product/wrapper YAML exists; not hardware verified.

preview_release_matrix.stable_blocker: LED preview-to-stable gauntlet (operator flash proof, bench verification, stable promotion)
preview_release_matrix.notes: Already published on the preview channel (status: preview, channel: preview, webflash_build_matrix: true). No LED-stable claim.

preview_release_matrix.stable_blocker: Mains-safety / installation-approval / creepage / clearance evidence + competent-person sign-off + GPIO3 strap-pin boot characterisation
preview_release_matrix.notes: Relay-switched fan. Buildable (validated-full-compile). Preview-eligible with warning; carries the relay/mains caveat. Not stable, not recommended, not default.

preview_release_matrix.stable_blocker: Measured current / thermal evidence (S360-311-CURRENT-THERMAL-001); RPM/TachIO not claimed (rpm_supported: false)
preview_release_matrix.notes: Native ESP32-S3 GPIO PWM path is compile-proven. Current/thermal not measured; RPM not claimed. Preview-eligible with warning. Legacy SX1509 path remains superseded.

preview_release_matrix.stable_blocker: Cloudlift S12 / J3 harness + product-bench evidence; S360-312 schematic / BOM
preview_release_matrix.notes: 0-10V DAC fan control. Buildable (validated-full-compile); enforces FanDAC <-> AirIQ mutex. Not Cloudlift-ready. Preview-eligible with warning. No compliance claim.

preview_release_matrix.stable_blocker: PACKAGE-TRIAC-001 (operator bench protocol with signed attestation) + experimental-lane preconditions per COMPLIANCE-001-RESOLUTION-001 (COMPLIANCE-001 CLOSED by market posture — never placed on the market; any future market placement reopens it and requires external safety + EMC assessment first; see docs/decisions/COMPLIANCE-001-RESOLUTION-001.md). HW-005 buildability was resolved by TRIAC-PINMAP-CORRECT-001.
preview_release_matrix.notes: Mains-voltage TRIAC phase control. Advanced-preview ONLY: never stable, never recommended, never default. Requires the advanced mains-risk warning copy. Built (when published) on the preview build channel with the advanced acknowledgement gate. HW-005 buildability is resolved (TRIAC-PINMAP-CORRECT-001); publishing stays gated by PACKAGE-TRIAC-001 + the COMPLIANCE-001-RESOLUTION-001 experimental-lane preconditions (COMPLIANCE-001 closed by posture — S360-320 is never placed on the market; self-build open-source only). Moving FanTRIAC into the experimental_lane is the separate, human-reviewed commissioning PR.

rescue_ownership.note: Rescue firmware is WebFlash-owned and tracked separately. It is not a Sense360 release-channel product and is out of scope for this policy.
